_meta.author: Claude (overnight va-legislature-refine autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 32-36 (Shenandoah Valley / NW Virginia), state tier. Bill Wiley (HD-32, R), Justin Pence (HD-33, R), Tony Wilt (HD-34, R), Chris Runion (HD-35, R), Ellen McLaughlin (HD-36, R). Evidence-based finalization from LIS roll calls (HJ9/HJ3 marriage amendment; HJ1/SJ247 reproductive-freedom amendment), ReproRising VA scorecard, patroned bills, and the members' own campaign-issue statements. NO documented cross-party deviations found for any of the five (none among the GOP marriage-amendment crossovers). All cells with no public evidence left null; nothing inferred from party.

profile.religion: Attends Christ Episcopal Church, Winchester (continuing Anglican/Episcopal parish)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-32 (Winchester City + parts of Frederick & Warren Counties); in the House since 2020 (formerly HD-29, redistricted to HD-32 in 2024). Business development manager (Howard Shockey & Sons) and commercial real-estate broker; George Mason University grad; former Winchester City Council member (President 2018) and Planning Commission chairman. Re-elected Nov 4, 2025; term through Jan 2028.

bill-wiley.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-32 (Winchester/Frederick/Warren); in House since 2020. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative HARD record with NO documented party deviations: graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025; voted NAY on HJ1 (2026, reproductive-freedom amendment); voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment at BOTH references (HJ9 2025 and HJ3 2026) and was NOT among the GOP crossovers. Patroned HB1441 (proof-of-citizenship to register to vote) and HB1569 (lower concealed-handgun-permit fee). Public pro-law-enforcement campaign position. Family/child, Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, border, and most economic cells have no isolated public record and are left null pending a cited vote.

0.note: Graded F/'Extremist' (most pro-life) by ReproRising 2025; voted NAY on HJ1 (2026), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34).
Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment at both references (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026) — affirms the existing one-man/one-woman definition; not among the GOP crossovers.

1.note: Voted NAY on HJ3 (2026), the amendment to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Patroned HB1441 (2025) requiring documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote — citizenship-verification position.

0.note: Public campaign position backing law enforcement and opposing defunding police.

1.note: Patroned HB1569 (2025) lowering the concealed-handgun-permit fee (reduces barriers to lawful carry); stated Second Amendment support.

profile.religion: Methodist (per official VA House member biography)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-33 (Shenandoah County + parts of Rockingham, Page, Warren); first elected Nov 2025, seated Jan 14, 2026, succeeding former Speaker Todd Gilbert. Beef-cattle farmer and ATM-management-company COO; Virginia Tech (Animal & Poultry Science); longtime Virginia Farm Bureau board member. Brand-new member — thin voting record by design.

justin-pence.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-33 (Shenandoah Valley); first-year member, seated Jan 14, 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] As a 2026 freshman his record exists only for the second-reference amendments: voted NAY on HJ3 (same-sex marriage) — NOT among the only three GOP crossovers (Bloxom, McNamara, Garrett) — and NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment on the party-line 64-34 vote. Campaign positions: 'No Boys in Girls sports,' parental influence on classroom material, strong Second Amendment supporter and NRA Lifetime Member. Many cells (Christian liberty, public justice, federal overreach, border, economic) are genuinely no-evidence-found given his short tenure and are left null.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1/HJR1, 2026), which passed the House 64-34 on a party-line vote with no Republican crossovers — pro-life position.
Voted NAY on HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment; NOT among the only three GOP crossovers (Bloxom, McNamara, Garrett) — affirms one-man/one-woman definition.

1.note: Voted NAY on enshrining same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3, 2026).

2.note: Campaign position: 'No Boys in Girls sports. Period.' — affirms biological sex in school athletics.

1.note: Self-described 'strong Second Amendment supporter,' 'a law-abiding citizen ought to be able to have whatever gun they want,' and NRA Lifetime Member — opposes gun restrictions.

profile.religion: Christian; leader at Truth and Grace Ministries; B.A. in applied ministry, Cornerstone Bible College (2005)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-34 (Harrisonburg City + part of Rockingham County); in the House since a June 2010 special election (formerly HD-26, redistricted to HD-34 in 2024). Retired small-business owner (Superior Concrete); former Chairman of the House Public Safety Committee; past president, Harrisonburg/Rockingham Chamber of Commerce. Re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (52.3%).

tony-wilt.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-34 (Harrisonburg/Rockingham); in House since 2010. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long-serving consistent conservative; NO documented party deviations. Voted NO on SJ247 (2025) reproductive-freedom amendment and graded F/'Extremist' by ReproRising 2025; in the 31-member GOP NO bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not among the named crossovers) at both references. Chaired the House Public Safety Committee (affirmative pro-law-enforcement role) and is an NRA Life Member on the Firearms subcommittee. Patroned HB2353 (minor's discharge plan sent to parents — parental notification) and HB2354 (facilitating ICE custody transfers — anti-sanctuary cooperation). Individual LIS line on the HJ9/HJ3 marriage roll calls not machine-confirmed this session (established by named-crossover exclusion); cells without a clean public record left null.

0.note: Voted NO on SJ247 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (party-line); graded F/'Extremist' (most pro-life) by ReproRising 2025.
In the 31-member GOP NO bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment at both references; not among the named Republican crossovers — affirms one-man/one-woman definition.

1.note: Voted against enshrining same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3, 2026 second reference) — not among the three GOP crossovers.
Patroned HB2353 (2025) requiring a minor's discharge plan be sent to parents/schools — parental notification on a minor's care.

0.note: Former Chairman of the House Public Safety Committee — affirmative pro-law-enforcement leadership role.

2.note: Patroned HB2354 (2025) facilitating transfer of certain incarcerated persons to ICE custody — cooperation-with-ICE / anti-sanctuary measure.

1.note: NRA Life Member; long service on the House Public Safety Firearms subcommittee — pro-Second-Amendment record.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-35 (Highland, Bath, parts of Augusta & Rockingham); in the House since Jan 2020 (formerly HD-25, redistricted to HD-35 in 2024). President & CEO of Eddie Edwards Signs; accounting degree (Virginia Tech) + MBA (JMU); sits on House Finance, Labor & Commerce, and Agriculture committees. Re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (72.4%); term through Jan 2028.

chris-runion.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-35 (Shenandoah Valley); in House since 2020. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative with the richest documented issue record of this batch and NO party deviations. HARD votes: NAY on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment (individually named in the NAYS), and NAY on HJ1 (2025) reproductive-freedom amendment. Campaign-stated positions: life begins at conception and 'work towards ending abortion in Virginia'; opposes gender-identity/CRT 'extreme ideologies' in schools; supports reinstating voter ID and ending mass mail-in voting; supports Constitutional Carry and is an NRA/VCDL/NAGR member. Christian-liberty, public-justice, federal-overreach, and most economic/border cells have no isolated record and are left null.

0.note: Voted NAY on HJ1 (2025) reproductive-freedom amendment; campaign: 'I believe that life begins at conception.'

1.note: Campaign: 'I will continue to work towards ending abortion in Virginia' — advocates abolition, not merely restrictions.

0.note: Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment at both references (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026), individually named in the NAYS — affirms one-man/one-woman definition.

1.note: Voted NAY on HJ3 (2026) enshrining same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Campaign: opposes 'extreme ideologies related to gender identity' taught to youth — 'This needs to stop.'

4.note: Opposes promotion of gender-identity ideology in public schools (campaign issues page).

2.note: Opposes CRT and gender-identity curricula in schools; campaign explicitly says tax dollars spent teaching these ideologies 'needs to stop.'

1.note: Campaign: supports 'reinstating voter identification' and signature verification on absentee ballots.
Self-described 'champion of the Second Amendment'; member of NRA, VCDL, and NAGR — opposes gun restrictions.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-36 (Staunton, Waynesboro, parts of Augusta & Rockbridge); first seated Jan 11, 2023 via special election after the death of her husband Del. Ronnie Campbell (formerly Ellen H. Campbell; ran old 24th 2023-24, then HD-36). B.A. Averett University; financial professional; sits on House Appropriations. Re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (57.6%); term through 2028. Relatively new member (~3 years).

ellen-mclaughlin.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-36 (Augusta/Staunton/Waynesboro); seated Jan 2023 (formerly Ellen Campbell). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Short-tenure member with a legislative footprint heavy on healthcare/appropriations and commending resolutions rather than culture-war sponsorship. HARD primary-source votes secured: NAY on HJ1 (2026) reproductive-freedom amendment and NAY on HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment. No documented cross-party deviation (a challenger cited VPAP at ~93% party-line, SOFT). Family/child, Christian-liberty, election, public-justice, federal-overreach, border, economic, and self-defense cells are genuine no-evidence-found and left null — sparse but honest.

0.note: Recorded in the NAYS on HJ1 (2026), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34) — pro-life position.
Recorded in the NAYS on HJ3 (2026), the same-sex-marriage amendment (House 67-31) — affirms one-man/one-woman definition.

1.note: Voted NAY on enshrining same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3, 2026).
